page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture with ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The processor includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The server achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng score of 96246.73 and a top-tier PassMark memory score of 4657.57, supported by a read-only cached memory bandwidth of 6318.61 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 36.5 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression achieving 17.34 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, as demonstrated by a top-tier PassMark database score of 46541.54 and Redis SET operations processing 136.39 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also highly optimized at 104.04 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation performance is a relative bottleneck, with text generation on a small 135M model registering at a poor 31.56 tokens per second, despite strong prompt processing speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m9gd.12xlarge offers a highly dense resource profile, combining dedicated ARM64 compute cores, substantial memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage without the overhead of external storage attachments. This makes it highly suitable for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing workloads. It is less suited for workloads requiring specialized x86 vector instructions or real-time large language model text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with top-tier results in Redis SET operations at 136.39 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 104.04 GB/sec. While single-threaded compression and database operations perform in the top tier, LLM text generation remains a relative bottleneck. This server is highly cost-efficient for high-throughput databases, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads that benefit from localized NVMe storage and dedicated ARM64 architecture.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server with 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance combining dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and exceptional memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processors at 2.8 GHz.
192.0 GB of system memory paired with 2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier performance in Redis operations, database tasks, and static web serving.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth delivered via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for high-throughput databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing.
